body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve JSON types when converting Anthropic tool inputs**

The converter stores every non-string argument as `GetRawText()` and then re-serializes the dictionary, which turns numbers/booleans/arrays/objects into JSON strings. That breaks tool execution for typed parameters (for example `append: true` becomes `"true"` and `FileSystemService.GetBool` treats it as false, and array/object args like `keys`/`tasks` no longer parse as arrays). This can cause incorrect or failed tool actions whenever Claude emits non-string tool arguments.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Filter system-control tools by the exact enabled category**

This block is intended to list only enabled categories, but when either `keyboard` or `mouse` is enabled it advertises the full combined tool list (including disabled-category tools). In text-mode providers, this prompt is the model’s tool contract, so users who enable only one input category can still be prompted toward disallowed tool calls.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Fixed in af8fedc. Rewrote `ConvertAnthropicToolUseToToolCall` to use `Utf8JsonWriter` + `JsonProperty.WriteTo()` instead of a `Dictionary<string, object?>`. This preserves the exact JSON type for every parameter — booleans stay booleans, numbers stay numbers, arrays stay arrays.

body: Fixed in af8fedc. The single combined line is now split: `take_screenshot` is always included when either category is enabled, keyboard tools (`begin_keyboard_session`, `end_keyboard_session`, `key_combo`, `key_press`, `type_text`) only when `hasKeyboard`, and mouse tools only when `hasMouse`.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Guard filesystem guidance in compact native prompt**

`BuildNativeCompact` always includes the scripting and large-file rules, which explicitly reference `write_file`, `execute_command`, and `read_file` even when `enabledCategories` excludes `filesystem`. In those browser-only/agent-only configurations, providers still filter tool schemas by category, so the prompt can direct OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini models toward tools that are not actually exposed, leading to invalid tool calls and stalled workflows.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Hide switch_model guidance when agent category is disabled**

The prompt builder computes `hasAgent` and conditionally renders the agent tool list, but the `switch_model` section is appended unconditionally. When users disable the `agent` category, tool schemas no longer include `switch_model`, yet the system prompt still advertises it as available, which can make the model emit an unavailable tool call.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
Fixed in 943b6e2. SCRIPTING and LARGE FILES rules in `BuildNativeCompact` are now gated on `hasFs` — they only appear when the filesystem category is enabled.

body: Fixed in 943b6e2. The `switch_model` line in `BuildTextBased` is now wrapped in `if (hasAgent)`, consistent with the tool schema filtering.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Guard additional browser/fs rules by enabled categories**

These rules are appended unconditionally and mention `open_url`, `open_browser_tab`, and `execute_command` even when browser/filesystem categories are disabled. In category-restricted configurations (such as system-only or agent-only), the prompt advertises tools that are not actually exposed, which can drive invalid tool calls.
